title: Home
seo_title: Wildwood Psychotherapy | Trauma-Informed Therapy in Leesburg, VA

blocks.hero_title: EMDR and Trauma-Informed Therapy for Adults and Couples in Leesburg, VA
blocks.hero_subtitle: You spend your life taking care of everyone else. It’s time to take care of YOU.

blocks.title: You’re exhausted from managing everyone else's expectations while your own needs sit on the back burner.
blocks.body:
Maybe you've noticed you're drinking more than you used to. Or working longer hours to avoid feeling what's underneath. Or your relationship is struggling because old patterns keep showing up in new ways. You know something has to change. You're here because you're finally ready to figure out what that something is.

I'm Kaylie, and I specialize in helping adults and couples in Leesburg, Virginia unpack the trauma that's been running the show. Together, we'll work through the anxiety, depression, and attachment wounds that keep you stuck in cycles you desperately want to break.

Underneath the surface, the weight of holding it all together is starting to feel heavy. At Wildwood, we move beyond traditional talk therapy. By integrating EMDR and IFS-informed parts work, we help you process the roots of your exhaustion so you can finally stop holding it together and start *living*. This is your space to be human, not just productive.
blocks.title: A Sanctuary for the Over-Functioner
When the Past Stays Present
blocks.subtitle: Understanding the subtle reach of developmental trauma
blocks.body: Trauma doesn’t always announce itself with a single, dramatic event. More often, it lives in the quiet, persistent ways you’ve learned to navigate a world that didn't feel safe. It’s the invisible architecture of your daily life, showing up in patterns like these:

items.title: Constantly feeling like you're waiting for the other shoe to drop.
items.content: Even when life is fairly predictable or even calm, your nervous system is scanning for a crisis. It’s that subtle humming of anxiety in your chest or the inability to truly relax after a day of work. You’ve become an expert at pre-fixing problems that haven't even happened yet.

items.title: Struggling to separate your worth from your productivity.
items.content: It feels like you have to be high-performing just to be worthy of being in the room. You might be the person everyone leans on, but inside, you’re terrified that if you slow down or make a mistake, the whole facade will crumble and people will see you the way *you* see you.

items.title: Being an expert at reading the room ... at the expense of your own needs.
items.content: You're incredibly attuned to other people’s moods, shifting the way you show up to keep the peace or stay safe. You often leave interactions feeling exhausted, wondering if you actually liked the person or if you were just busy making sure *they* liked *you*.

items.title: Feeling like a "floating head," disconnected from your physical self.
items.content: When things get intense, you might feel numb or checked out, as if you’re watching your life through a thick pane of glass. It’s hard to know what you’re feeling in your body until you’re already at a breaking point or completely burnt out.

items.title: Carrying a quiet, heavy sense that you are fundamentally difficult or broken.
items.content: Beyond just low self-esteem, it’s a deep-seated belief that your needs are a burden to others. You apologize for things that aren't your fault and hesitate to ask for help because you don't want to take up too much space in the world.

items.title: Using "doing" to avoid "being" — keeping your world busy so you don't have to feel.
items.content: Whether it’s staying up late to work, the endless scroll on your phone, or that extra (extra) glass of wine to take the edge off, you have a toolkit of habits designed to keep your feelings at bay. It’s a constant race to stay one step ahead of a restlessness you can't quite name. You're exhausted from trying to outrun your own internal landscape.

blocks.body: They are the brilliant, instinctual ways you learned to protect yourself when the world felt like too much. But the strategies that ensured your survival *then* are often the very things standing in the way of your vitality *now*. You don't need to be fixed. You deserve to be unburdened.
blocks.image_alt: Peaceful wooded path as found in Leesburg, VA, representing the journey of trauma recovery at Wildwood Psychotherapy.
blocks.subtitle: A Note on Adaptation
blocks.title: These aren't character flaws.
Beyond the Surface: Healing That Holds
blocks.body: You’ve likely tried therapy before. Maybe it felt like venting without real progress, or your therapist was nice but didn't quite help you move the needle. At Wildwood, we move beyond just talking about the problem. We utilize a blend of evidence-based, deep-reaching tools to help you stop surviving and start thriving.

items.title: EMDR Therapy
items.text: Find a structured path to finally file away the memories and sensations that keep you stuck in survival mode.

items.title: IFS-Informed Parts Work
items.text: Understand the different parts of yourself, like the one that wants to be vulnerable and the one terrified of getting hurt, so we can create more internal harmony.

items.title: Somatic & Practical Tools
items.text: Gain the tools to regulate your nervous system in real-time, building a life that feels grounded and intentional.

blocks.body: ## This isn't about fixing you; you aren’t broken. It’s about healing the wounds that convinced you that you were.

items.title: Individual Adults
items.text: You’ve built a life that looks enviable from the outside, but you’re running on fumes. You’re the dependable one for everyone else, yet you feel like you’re losing the thread of your own identity. You’re ready to stop performing and start feeling like yourself again.

items.title: Couples + Partners
items.text: What used to be your sanctuary now feels like a regular source of tension. Whether you’re navigating years of unspoken resentment or shifting into a new relationship structure, the connection feels frayed. You want to move past the same old fights and find your way back to each other.

items.title: Unique Dynamics
items.text: You don't have many people you can talk to about your lifestyle who get it. You need a space where your relationship structure and desires aren't the problems to be solved, but the context we honor. You’re looking for a therapist who speaks your language so you can dive straight into the work.

blocks.title: A space for you to feel seen and understood.
blocks.body:
Most people come to Wildwood because they’re tired of band-aid therapy. You don’t need more coping skills for a life that’s burning you out; you need to address the root of the fire.

Whether we're using EMDR to process old trauma or navigating the complexities of ethical non-monogamy, my approach is grounded, direct, and deeply human. We skip the Therapy 101 explanations and dive straight into the work that actually shifts how you feel on a Tuesday morning.

### Boutique Care in the Heart of Loudoun County

Walk through the doors of our Leesburg office for an in-person session, or join virtually from anywhere in Virginia. Either way, you get a therapist who sees *you*, not a diagnosis.

You deserve more than a therapist who just listens and nods. You deserve a partner in your growth. My approach is warm and grounded, but I’m not afraid to be direct. I’ll challenge the patterns that no longer serve you and help you connect the dots between your past and your present.

I believe that real healing happens in the space between us. That’s why I’m committed to showing up as a real human in our sessions, bringing clinical expertise, specialized trauma training, and a deep respect for the work you're doing.
blocks.link_text: Learn more about my background and certifications
blocks.subtitle: Owner, Board-Certified Licensed Professional Counselor, EMDR Certified Therapist
blocks.title: I'm Kaylie.

items.title: Trauma + EMDR
items.text: Move beyond simply talking about your past. Using EMDR, we help your brain reprocess and integrate stuck memories so they stop hijacking your present and start becoming part of your story — not your identity.

items.title: ENM + Polyamorous Relationships
items.text: Navigate jealousy, boundaries, and complex communication in a space that honors your structure. Work with a therapist who respects and understands the nuance of non-traditional dynamics without you having to explain the basics.

items.title: Anxiety & Depression
items.text: Break the cycle of high-functioning exhaustion. Shift from a state of constant survival to one of intentional regulation, giving your nervous system the quiet it needs to finally feel like yourself again.

items.title: Consultation
items.text: Schedule a free 15-minute phone consultation. We'll talk about what you're dealing with and whether we're a good fit.

items.title: Intake
items.text: Book your first session if it feels right. No pressure, no sales pitch.

items.title: Sessions
items.text: Begin the work of understanding yourself, healing old wounds, and building a life that aligns with your values and desires.

blocks.title: You don't have to struggle alone.
blocks.body:
Life can sometimes feel unbearable. Maybe you’ve tried dealing with your issues by talking to close friends, searching internet rabbit holes, compartmentalizing, and/or distracting yourself with things like scrolling, substances, or spending.

Wherever you go, though, there you are. The issue remains, and though you may have had temporary relief, you feel more alone, even shameful, afterward.

Whether you’re experiencing new challenges or iterations of long-standing ones, therapy can help. You deserve to feel seen, heard, and supported. You can have a more balanced, joyful existence.

**Healing is possible, and I can help.**

blocks.tagline: TAKE A BREATH. YOU’RE IN THE RIGHT PLACE.
blocks.title: THIS IS WHERE CHANGE BEGINS.
